sections.name: Ant Matutinum 3N
lines: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, thou that killest the prophets.

sections.name: Responsory4
lines: R. But Stephen, being full of the Holy Ghost, looking up steadfastly to heaven, saw the glory of God,
* And Jesus standing on the right hand of God.
V. And crying out in a loud voice he said: Behold, I see the heavens opened.
R. And Jesus standing on the right hand of God.

sections.name: Lectio6
lines: @Sancti/12-26:Lectio5:s/Yesterday Christ.*//s

sections.name: Lectio7
lines: @Sancti/12-26:Lectio5:s/.* Yesterday Christ/Yesterday Christ/s s/$/~/
@Sancti/12-26:Lectio6:s/Stephen, .*//s

sections.name: Lectio8
lines: @Sancti/12-26:Lectio6:s/.* Stephen, /Stephen, /s

sections.name: Responsory8
lines: R. Now there arose some of that synagogue disputing with Stephen.
* And they were not able to resist the wisdom and the spirit that spoke.
V. And they stirred up the people and running together, they took him, and brought him to the council.
R. And they were not able to resist the wisdom and the spirit that spoke.

sections.name: Lectio10
lines: @Sancti/12-26:Lectio8:s/Secondly, .*//s

sections.name: Lectio11
lines: @Sancti/12-26:Lectio8:s/.* Secondly, /Secondly, /s s/$/~/
@Sancti/12-26:Lectio9:s/Against .*//s

sections.name: Responsory11
lines: R. Yesterday the Lord was born on earth, so that today Stephen can be born in heaven. The Lord entered the world
* So that Stephen could enter heaven.
V. For yesterday our King was clothed in robe of flesh going forth from the courtyard of the virginal womb he deigned to visit the world.
R. So that Stephen could enter heaven.

sections.name: Lectio12
lines: @Sancti/12-26:Lectio9:s/.* Against /Against /s

sections.name: Responsory12
lines: R. Devout men buried Stephen and made great mourning over him.
* There was raised a great persecution against the church which was at Jerusalem.
V. Saul was consenting to his death, made havock of the church
R. There was raised a great persecution against the church which was at Jerusalem.
